rulings.Case Title: RUDOLF V ISCH VS. VINCENT A ISCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Judgment Obtained By Petitioner Rudolf V. Isch
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 2, 2015, Line 7, Motion To Set Aside Judgment Obtained By Petitioner Rudolf V. Isch. Motion denied. First, Respondent has presented no factual basis to conclude that the judgment or the order he seeks to set aside are void. Second, Respondent has failed to articulate a satisfactory excuse for failing to present a defense. Respondent failed to appear at the arbitration hearing on time, and asserts that on this basis he was given false information and denied an opportunity to participate in the hearing. However, relief from extrinsic mistake is denied where the complaining party has contributed to the mistake giving rise to the judgment thus obtained. (Hammell v. Britton (1941) 19 Cal.2d 72, 80.) Further, Respondent's contention that his attorney became incapacitated and unable to timely file his petition is not supported by Vetesi's Declaration or any other evidence presented, and is undermined by the very arguments made by Vetesi during the hearing on the Petition to Confirm the Arbitration Award. Finally, observations made by and conclusions drawn by Respondent's attorneys as to his mental state are insufficient to demonstrate that Respondent suffered from a mental disability that prevented him from presenting his case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defts' Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents Set Two, Nos. 108-110, 113-115, And 117 And For Compliance With Notice Of Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, January 2, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER's Motion To Compel Deftendants' Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents Set Two, Nos. 108-110, 113-115, And 117 And For Compliance With Notice Of Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court's tentative ruling, subject to further explanation by the plaintiff, is to sustain defendants' objections to the document request on the ground of relevancy, overbreadth, burden and third party privacy rights. The parties shall report on the status of the PMK deposition which is also a subject of this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COSIMO PARENTI VS. FARINA FOCACCIA CUCINA ITALIANA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 2, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT FARINA FOCACCIA CUCINA ITALIANA LLC Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. Granted pursuant to CCP sec. 473(b). Defendant shall file and serve its proposed answer within ten days of entry of this Order. Policy favors a trial on the merits. (Rosenthal v. Garner (1983) 142 Cal.App.3d 891, 898.) The Declaration of Luca Minna establishes inadvertence or excusable neglect. The complaint was served on one of Defendant's employees who mislaid the complaint with other papers, rather than forward it to Defendant's agent for service of process. The motion was timely filed within six months of entry of default and default judgment because October 4, 2014 (six months from the date of entry of default) was on a Saturday. This motion was filed on October 6, 2014, the following Monday. The motion was filed within a reasonable period of time after Defendant dismissed its appeal. Although a satisfaction of judgment has been filed, this does not preclude Defendant from setting aside the default and default judgment. (See Patterson v. Keeney (1913) 165 Cal. 465, 466.) Plaintiff cites no authority to support its argument that Defendant cannot move to set aside the default because it appealed the entry of default. Even if this did constitute a general appearance, it occurred after default was entered. Lastly, evidence submitted in reply establishes that Defendant is authorized to conduct business in California. (See Declaration of Erica McDowell, par. 6.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DESOTO CAB COMPANY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. DARNICE LINTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Disovery Responses And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,925
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, January 2, 2015, Line 4, APPELLANT DESOTO CAB COMPANY, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Disovery Responses And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,925. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court and parties to discuss the need/scope of a discovery plan relative to the specific issues raised for determination of this Labor Code section 98.2 action. See Sales Dimensions vs. Superior Court (1979) 90 Cal. App 3d. 757. The parties to report on whether the documentary evidence from the Department of Labor administrative hearing is presently equally available to all sides. Also to be discussed is assignment of the action to a Single Assignment Department. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)
